text: The spare room is lit blue by a single wide monitor, a yoke and throttle quadrant clamped to a small desk, a lamp on low behind him.
Birdy sits close to the screen, one hand on the yoke, the other walking the levers up.
Flaps five, set. Bugs are set.
On the monitor a Q400 sits at the hold short line, runway lights running out into the dark ahead.
Flapjack twenty-two sixty, runway two seven, cleared for takeoff, wind two five zero at eight.
Twenty-two sixty, cleared for takeoff, two seven.
He eases the throttles forward and the engine note climbs on the speakers.
Power's set. Airspeed alive.
The runway lights start to slide toward the screen.
Eighty knots, checks.
V1. Rotate.
He draws the yoke back a small even amount and the runway drops away under the nose.
Positive rate.
His thumb finds a switch by feel without looking.
Gear up.
Maya stands in the doorway in a robe, a glass of water in her hand.
I put the fan on in the bedroom. It's warm back there.
Yeah. Okay.
He doesn't turn from the screen. The turboprop banks slow and level.
Flaps up, climb power set.
There's the rest of the pasta if you want it. It's in the blue thing.
I'm alright. I ate.
You didn't, but okay.
She sips the water and watches the screen a moment.
Which one's this.
The Dash. Same one from work.
It looks the same as the other one to me.
It's the same, yeah.
The aircraft settles into a steady climb, the wings holding flat.
Don't be too late. You've got the early one.
I won't. Just this leg.
She stands there another second. He runs a hand across the panel and clicks something.
Passing through six thousand.
Okay. Night.
Night. I'll be in.
Maya goes out. Her footsteps go down the hall and a door closes soft.
Birdy flies on alone, calling the levels under his breath, sure and quiet.
Level change, ten thousand. Speed two ten.
Time cuts. The clock on the desk reads later. The engine hum still plays low from the speakers.
Birdy is slumped back in the chair, chin down, asleep, one hand still resting on the yoke.
On the monitor the Q400 holds a straight and level cruise above a dark map, the autopilot flying it.
Maya comes back in with a folded blanket. She stops at the doorway, looks at him.
She lays the blanket over his chest and legs and tucks the edge behind his shoulder.
She lifts his hand off the yoke and sets it in his lap. He doesn't wake.
She reaches past him and turns the lamp off. The blue screen keeps glowing on his face.
She leaves it running, goes out, and pulls the door most of the way shut.
